timeline_html:
Summary. Irina has experienced shedding that began more than a year ago and has tended to come in waves. Several stress-related and hormonal factors appear to have influenced her pattern over time.

Your hair-loss triggers.

  • Chronic stress (ongoing; started Jan 2018)
  • Rapid weight loss / restrictive dieting (resolved; Mar–Aug 2025)
  • Major emotional stress (resolved; Jan 2001–Aug 2023)
  • Started or stopped birth control / hormonal treatment (resolved; 2005–2012)
  • Other – Histamin intolerance (ongoing; started Jan 2014)

Timeline forecast. The wave of shedding tied to the 2025 weight change likely reached its worst point around May–Jun 2025. From there, shedding would be expected to settle through late 2025 and early 2026, with early regrowth visible by spring 2026. Because chronic stress and histamine intolerance are still active, some fluctuations may persist longer than this forecast. Dominant driver: Chronic stress since 2018 remains the main background factor, but the 2025 weight loss episode best explains the recent peaks in shedding.

Where you are now. You’re in the stabilization to early regrowth phase—shedding should be easing, and the baby hairs you’ve seen are a positive sign that recovery is underway. Keep supporting your stress balance and scalp health; steadier months are ahead. 🌱
